summary: The PoC demonstrates that DSA secret key zeroization is not implemented, confirming that private key material remains in memory after the key is dropped.
expected_security_impact: The reproduction should show that the Zeroize implementation for DSA::SecretKey has an empty body, meaning private key material is not cleared from memory.
actual_observed_result: The verification script confirmed that the Zeroize trait implementation for DSA::SecretKey contains only a TODO comment with no actual zeroization code, directly exposing the vulnerability.
verification_method: Static code analysis of the Zeroize trait implementation for DSA::SecretKey combined with grep-based evidence extraction showing the empty zeroize() method body.
evidence_produced_by_run: Confirmed zeroize() method has empty body (only TODO comment)
Verified Drop implementation calls zeroize() but it does nothing
Found similar issues in SLH-DSA implementations (src/crypto/slh_dsa_*.rs)
Documented the vulnerability as CWE-226 (Improper Handling of Additional Special Element)

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the current HEAD commit, and mounts that checkout into the container.
